skill.name: benchling-integration
skill.description: Benchling R&D platform integration guidance for registry entities, inventory, ELN entries, workflows, apps, APIs, and Data Warehouse access.
skill.summary: Use Benchling SDK and REST API patterns for secure lab data automation.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Manual review found that the high static score is driven by Markdown examples for Benchling API usage, placeholder credentials, tenant URLs, and CSV export snippets. No malicious code, prompt injection attempt, real secret, or covert exfiltration endpoint was found. Publishable with normal caution because users may connect to sensitive Benchling data and credentials.

low_findings.title: Static command execution alerts are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The numerous backtick execution alerts occur in Markdown documentation and code fences. I did not find evidence that the skill package executes shell, Ruby, or Python commands itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation snippets and Markdown formatting, not executable package code. This is a strong false-positive signal after reviewing the surrounding context.

low_findings.title: Placeholder credential and environment examples
low_findings.description: The API key and environment variable alerts are instructional placeholders for Benchling authentication. They do not expose real secrets, but users must avoid copying real credentials into prompts or files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed values are placeholders and security guidance, not embedded credentials. The remaining risk is operational because the skill teaches real credential-based integrations.

low_findings.title: Network findings match expected Benchling API documentation
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL and fetch alerts point to Benchling tenant URL examples and REST API reference material. I did not find evidence of covert endpoints or unauthorized data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network locations are expected for an API integration skill and use Benchling documentation domains or tenant placeholders. No unrelated collection endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Filesystem write example is documentation only
low_findings.description: The file write alert comes from an example that exports sequence metadata to CSV. It is not executed by the skill, but copied scripts could write sensitive lab data locally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a documentation example, so the package does not perform a write operation. The caution is credible because Benchling data can be sensitive if users run adapted exports.

low_findings.title: Critical heuristic combination is not confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined documentation examples for commands, network calls, credential setup, and file export into a critical heuristic. Manual review did not find malicious intent, obfuscation, or prompt-injection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple risky tokens are present, but they are explained by reference documentation for a legitimate API integration. I found no semantic evidence of exfiltration, hidden execution, or audit bypass text.

content.value_statement: Benchling integrations can be hard to design because registry, inventory, ELN, workflow, and warehouse APIs use different patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical guidance for secure Benchling automation.
actual_capabilities: Explains Benchling API key and OAuth authentication patterns.
Guides registry operations for DNA, RNA, protein, custom entity, and mixture records.
Shows inventory workflows for samples, containers, locations, and transfers.
Supports ELN entry, workflow task, and Benchling App planning.
Provides REST endpoint and Python SDK reference guidance.
Covers Data Warehouse access patterns for reporting and analytics.
limitations: Requires a valid Benchling tenant and user-managed credentials.
Does not include executable automation scripts or a packaged connector.
Cannot bypass Benchling permissions, schemas, rate limits, or audit controls.
Examples must be adapted to each tenant schema and data governance policy.

use_cases.title: Registry import planning
use_cases.description: Map sequence files or entity metadata into Benchling registry create and update operations.

use_cases.title: Inventory workflow automation
use_cases.description: Design safe workflows for container lookup, transfer tracking, sample updates, and storage audits.

use_cases.title: R&D analytics extraction
use_cases.description: Plan Data Warehouse queries and API exports for compliance reports, dashboards, and operational metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Set up authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Benchling SDK authentication using environment variables for my tenant. Include the permissions I should verify first.

prompt_templates.title: Create registry records
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Benchling workflow to create DNA sequence records with custom schema fields and register them with a naming strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Audit inventory movement
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an inventory audit that lists containers in a location, checks barcodes, and prepares transfer updates for review.

prompt_templates.title: Build an analytics pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Benchling Data Warehouse and REST API pipeline for monthly compliance reporting with least-privilege access controls.

output_examples.input: How should I authenticate a Benchling automation script?
output: Use a tenant-specific URL and a credential stored outside source control.
Prefer API keys for personal scripts and OAuth client credentials for production apps.
Verify the account has only the required Benchling project, folder, and registry permissions.

output_examples.input: Can you help plan a container transfer workflow?
output: Start by listing source containers and validating destination storage locations.
Prepare transfer requests in batches and log every proposed move for human review.
Handle rate limits and failed updates with retry logic and clear status reporting.

output_examples.input: What should an analytics export include?
output: Define the reporting goal, target Benchling schemas, and approved fields first.
Use Data Warehouse access for broad reporting and REST calls for current operational records.
Exclude secrets, unnecessary personal data, and restricted experimental details.

best_practices: Store Benchling credentials in a secret manager or environment variables.
Use least-privilege app or service account permissions for automation.
Test create, update, transfer, and export flows in a non-production project first.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys, client secrets, or warehouse passwords into prompts.
Do not run bulk updates without dry-run output and review steps.
Do not export sensitive sequence, sample, or ELN data without policy approval.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Benchling by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is documentation and guidance for building integrations. Users provide their own tenant, credentials, and runtime environment.

faq.question: Which Benchling authentication methods are covered?
faq.answer: It covers API keys, OAuth client credentials, OIDC context, and secure credential handling patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with registry entities?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers DNA, RNA, protein, custom entity, mixture, schema field, and registration workflows.

faq.question: Can it automate inventory operations?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for samples, containers, locations, transfers, and inventory audits.

faq.question: Does it include Data Warehouse guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes how to use Benchling warehouse access for analytics, reporting, dashboards, and compliance queries.

faq.question: What security precautions should users follow?
faq.answer: Use least privilege, protect credentials, avoid unnecessary exports, and test workflows before running production changes.
